body:
## Problem

Surfaced during PR #491 review (Codex round 8). Reviewer-facing application/funding materials carry **stale and internally contradictory scale metrics**, and there is no single authoritative number to propagate from — even the designated source of truth disagrees with itself.

### Verified current numbers (2026-06-15)
From `repo-registry.json` + `scripts/v4-dependency-validation.py`:
- **150** total repos · **93** ACTIVE · **55** ARCHIVED · **3** PROTOTYPE
- **62** dependency edges · **1** back-edge (`styx-behavioral-commerce → organvm-ii-poiesis/styx-behavioral-art`, III→II)

### Drift observed
- `docs/applications/00-covenant-ark.md` (declared source of truth) says **both** "150 repositories" (L76) **and** "100 repos … total_repos → 100" (L214).
- `applications/shared/system-overview.md`: prose says "150 repositories" but Key Metrics say "**97 repositories**", "93 ACTIVE (100% of non-archived)" (false — non-archived ≈ 95), and "**zero back-edge violations**" (false — v4 reports 1).
- `applications/ai-systems-role.md`: "coordinates 150 repositories … and **zero back-edge violations**" (the back-edge claim is false).
- ~8 files match `97 repositories|zero back-edge`; ~30 application files are currently in flux on the PR branch from daily `auto-refresh metrics` commits that updated prose but not Key Metrics blocks.

## Why this is its own issue (not folded into #491)
- It is **pre-existing corpus-wide drift**, not introduced by #491's issue work (the auto-refresh commits merely rode the branch).
- It touches **reviewer-facing funding/job materials** — accuracy here is the owner's positioning call.
- The propagation generator is **deprecated** (`scripts/praxis-application-generator.py.deprecated`), so there is no live single-source propagation; reconciliation needs a decision + mechanism.

## Proposed work
1. **Pick one authoritative count** and fix `covenant-ark` to state it once (it currently self-contradicts).
2. Decide the honest framing of the **1 back-edge** for reviewers: either (a) remediate the styx III→II edge (route via Logos Bypass per `docs/standards/27`, removing the structural edge → genuinely zero back-edges), or (b) state "1 known back-edge, tracked" — **not** "zero".
3. Reconcile/recompute the derived counts (ACTIVE/archived/prototype, edge totals) and propagate to all application materials (revive or replace the deprecated generator, or a one-pass sweep with a checked-in metrics source).
4. Add a CI check (docs CI) that the advertised metrics match `repo-registry.json` / v4 output, to prevent re-drift.

Refs: PR #491 (where this surfaced), `docs/standards/27-logos-bypass-feedback-routing.md`, `docs/evaluation/organ-iii-multi-product-interference.md` §3/§6.
title: Corpus-wide metric reconciliation: repo-count drift (97/100/150) + false "zero back-edge" claim in reviewer-facing materials

body:
## Context

PR #491 (advancing the #94 CI/CD-templates epic) shipped two **bespoke regex-based linters** as reusable-workflow engines:

- `scripts/aesthetic_guard.py` — palette/typography contract checker (GH#102)
- `scripts/seed_gate.py` — seed.yaml contract validator (GH#94 Template 4)

During review they were hardened across 6 rounds (all findings fixed/resolved). They are correct and self-tested for the **common** authoring forms. But a regex heuristic over CSS/JS/Tailwind/YAML inherently can't cover every authoring style, so an automated reviewer can keep surfacing edge cases indefinitely. The **root-cause fix** is to stop hand-rolling parsers and delegate to real ones.

## Proposed work

**Aesthetics (replace `aesthetic_guard.py`):**
- `stylelint` with a custom config restricting colors/fonts to the `organ-aesthetic.yaml` palette (e.g. `color-no-hex` + allowlist, `declaration-property-value-allowed-list`).
- `eslint-plugin-tailwindcss` (with a restricted/locked theme) to enforce utility-class colors/fonts — the class of check the heuristic explicitly does **not** do.
- Keep `organ-aesthetic.yaml` as the source of truth; generate the stylelint/eslint config from it.

**Seed contracts (replace `seed_gate.py`):**
- Validate `seed.yaml` against the authoritative JSON Schema in `schema-definitions` (the doc the gate currently only approximates) using a standard validator (e.g. `ajv`/`check-jsonschema`).
- Keep the few cross-field invariants the schema can't express (no self-reference; `source: EXTERNAL` discipline per `docs/standards/29`) as a thin post-schema check.

## Documented limitations carried over from #491 (the deferred edge cases)

These are intentionally **not** handled by the current heuristics and are the concrete acceptance targets for the replacement:
- Tailwind **utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-sky-600`) — palette enforcement.
- CSS `font:` **shorthand** (`font: 16px 'X', sans-serif`) — primary-family extraction.
- Arbitrary multi-line Tailwind theme objects / SCSS maps / `@import`-ed fonts.
- Full per-entry schema validation of all seed sections (beyond the list/mapping shape checks already added).
- Reusable-workflow **engine-ref reproducibility**: a pinned consumer must currently set `engine-ref` explicitly; a self-referencing default would be cleaner.

## Scope / acceptance
- [ ] stylelint + eslint-plugin-tailwindcss configs generated from `organ-aesthetic.yaml`; wired into `reusable-aesthetic-guard.yml`.
- [ ] JSON-Schema validation wired into `reusable-registry-gate.yml`; bespoke `seed_gate.py` retired (or reduced to the cross-field invariants only).
- [ ] The deferred edge cases above are covered by the real tooling.
- [ ] `aesthetic_guard.py` / `seed_gate.py` either removed or clearly marked superseded (per the No-Deletion principle, std 23 — evolve, don't delete).

## Source
Follow-up to #94 (CI/CD templates epic) and #491 (review rounds 1–6). The heuristic engines remain in place and functional until this lands.
title: Replace bespoke aesthetic-guard / seed-gate linters with off-the-shelf tooling (follow-up to #94 / #491)

## Secret Scanning Alert

Our automated secret scanning has detected potential secrets or credentials in the codebase.

### Scan Results

- **TruffleHog**: ✅ Clean
- **Gitleaks**: ⚠️ Found 6862 potential leaks
- **detect-secrets**: ⚠️ Found 43 files with secrets

### Action Required

1. **Review the scan results** attached to this workflow run
2. **Identify false positives** and update .gitleaks.toml or .secrets.baseline if needed
3. **Rotate any real secrets** that were detected
4. **Remove secrets from git history** using tools like BFG Repo-Cleaner or git-filter-repo
5. **Update affected videos** if secrets appear in walkthrough recordings

### Resources

- [GitHub Secret Scanning Documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/secret-scanning)
- [Removing Sensitive Data from a Repository](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/removing-sensitive-data-from-a-repository)
- [BFG Repo-Cleaner](https://rtyley.github.io/bfg-repo-cleaner/)

**Workflow Run**: https://github.com/organvm-i-theoria/.github/actions/runs/27529818038

Track E (Professionalization / Institutional Auditor) rolling scoreboard. Doctrine: session-meta `escape-velocity/doctrine/TRACK-E-PROFESSIONALIZATION-2026-06-11.md` · packet: `escape-velocity/doctrine/packets/STREAM-PROF-PACKET-2026-06-12.md` (re-issued daily by HELM).

**Mission**: make the corpus legible. Per repo: seven questions → state (`active|incubation|inquiry|archive|retired`) → surface verdict (`professional|needs-polish|internal-only|archive`) → stranger-60s sentence → ONE `PROFESSIONALIZATION:` issue on the audited repo → audit row in `professionalization/<org>/REPO_AUDIT.md`.

**Scoreboard protocol** (PROF sessions post here):
- one cumulative scoreboard comment every 10 audited repos: totals by state × surface verdict, last repo, next repo, remaining by org, failures
- final stopping comment at boundary completion or UID exhaustion, with resume command

**Pilot boundary**: organvm-iii-ergon (packet default — the commercial face; conductor may override). HELM spot-verifies a sample of pilot verdicts before authorizing wave 2.

**Audience tests**: can a stranger / a buyer / a collaborator / future-me understand it?
**Posture law**: surfaces must communicate *competent owner of systems*, never simulated engineering depth.

LABELS_REQUESTED: professionalization, track-e

— opened by HELM 0fc455b5 under claim https://github.com/4444J99/session-meta/issues/10#issuecomment-4691327385 (receipt EV-2026-06-12-200000)

**Identity:** Private git superproject umbrella for ORGAN-VII (Kerygma) aggregating five POSSE content-distribution submodules (announcement-templates, social-automation, distribution-strategy, kerygma-profiles, kerygma-pipeline) plus .github via gitlink pointers, with a cross-repo validate.sh.

**Frozen state:** built-never-deployed. Main froze 2026-03-04 (d0c2f954, "chore: checkpoint all local changes"); gitlinks pinned at Feb 27 - Mar 4 SHAs (announcement-templates 823940d5, kerygma-pipeline 7352cad7, kerygma-profiles abc16d91 — all date-verified this run).

**Shipped test:** FAIL. No user-reachable path: repo is private (org-members only); declared homepage https://organvm-vii-kerygma.github.io/organvm-vii-kerygma--superproject/ re-probed this run — HTTP 404. The documented `git clone --recurse-submodules` path works for the authenticated owner only (all six pinned gitlink SHAs verified to still resolve post-transfer via GitHub redirect), which is not a shipped path.

**Evidence level:** runnable-from-repo (authenticated recursive clone succeeded in the audit run; verifier independently confirmed every pinned gitlink SHA still resolves, so the clone reproduces a coherent Feb/Mar snapshot).

**Verdict:** PARK (verifier concurred after adversarial re-check; verdict unchanged).

**Minimal activation path:**
1. Decide the structural question first: whether the ORGAN-VII umbrella lives here or follows its five content submodules into a-organvm (all five are public at a-organvm/<name>, pushed 2026-05-31; no superseding umbrella exists in a-organvm — verified this run).
2. If kept here: rewrite all six URLs in `.gitmodules` from `git@github.com:organvm-vii-kerygma/<name>.git` (and the one https organvm-vii-kerygma URL) to `https://github.com/a-organvm/<name>.git`, removing dependence on transfer redirects and SSH keys.
3. Merge or close branch `chore/platform-standards-audit` (11 ahead / 0 behind main, touching all submodule pointers + `tools/audit_platform_standards.sh`) to refresh stale gitlink SHAs.
4. Fix doc drift: add the missing `kerygma-profiles` entry to `README-superproject.md` (registered 2026-02-24, never documented) and extend `validate.sh` (currently covers 3 of 5 content submodules — deliberately skips kerygma-pipeline, omits kerygma-profiles entirely). Clear or fix the dead homepage URL in repo settings.

**Reason for PARK:** Submodule repos migrated to a-organvm and moved on (heads Apr 23 - May 29) while this umbrella's main froze 2026-03-04 with stale pointers, phantom-org URLs, a 404 homepage, and an unmerged 11-commit sync branch; it works mechanically for the owner but ships nothing to anyone. Not KILL: it is the sole ORGAN-VII aggregation surface, no replacement umbrella exists, and open issue #5 (Kerygma Pipeline Activation epic) shows standing intent — the blocker is the unsettled org-vs-a-organvm consolidation decision, not dead code.

**PR candidate (not opened this run):** ~12-line fix in two files: (1) `.gitmodules` — rewrite all six submodule URLs from `git@github.com:organvm-vii-kerygma/<name>.git` (and the one https organvm-vii-kerygma URL) to `https://github.com/a-organvm/<name>.git`, removing dependence on transfer redirects and SSH keys; (2) `README-superproject.md` — add the missing kerygma-profiles entry to the submodule list (registered 2026-02-24, commit d8f62338, never documented). Optionally clear the dead homepage URL via repo settings. Does not fix stale gitlink SHAs (that is the unmerged chore/platform-standards-audit branch's job).

Cursor: organvm-vii-kerygma wave2 kerygma 1/2 · session 9aa974a8 · tracking: a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm#484
title: ACTIVATION AUDIT: park

**Frozen-state classification:** actually-live

**Shipped test:** PASS — org profile renders at https://github.com/organvm-vii-kerygma (profile/README.md) and the Pages landing https://organvm-vii-kerygma.github.io/.github/ returned HTTP 200 on curl -sI probes (re-verified this run). The embedded pipeline path does NOT pass: all 8 functional workflows (ci-pipeline, dispatch-log, dispatch-receiver, notify-kerygma, quarterly-feedback, quarterly-synthesis-dispatch, rss-auto-dispatch, weekly-analytics) are disabled_manually and dispatch-receiver.yml has zero runs ever.

**Evidence level:** live-url-confirmed (both URLs independently re-probed HTTP 200 by verifier)

**Verdict:** ship-now

**Minimal activation path:**
1. Nothing required for the meta-repo role — org profile and Pages landing are already user-reachable (probed HTTP 200 this run).
2. Optional hygiene: update `profile/README.md` repo table — the three listed repos (social-automation, distribution-strategy, announcement-templates) were transferred to org a-organvm; links currently work only via GitHub 301 redirect (verified this run) and the "4 repositories · 3 SKELETON · 1 DESIGN_ONLY" claim is stale.
3. Optional hygiene: update `seed.yaml` (implementation_status: PRODUCTION, packages: block) to reflect that pipeline packages now live in a-organvm under the private organvm-vii-kerygma--superproject; the in-repo pipeline workflows remain intentionally disabled (do not re-enable — secrets KERYGMA_MASTODON_ACCESS_TOKEN, KERYGMA_DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL, KERYGMA_BLUESKY_HANDLE, KERYGMA_BLUESKY_APP_PASSWORD, KERYGMA_GHOST_API_URL, KERYGMA_GHOST_ADMIN_API_KEY, ORCHESTRATION_PAT would be needed and the last scheduled run failed 2026-03-04).

**PR candidate (not opened this run):** Edit profile/README.md repository table (~10 lines): point the three repo links at their post-transfer homes under github.com/a-organvm/ (announcement-templates, social-automation, distribution-strategy), and correct the stale "4 repositories · 3 SKELETON · 1 DESIGN_ONLY" count line to match the current org contents (.github, organvm-vii-kerygma.github.io, organvm-vii-kerygma--superproject). Pure text edit, no behavior change; fixes the only publicly visible defect.

body:
**Identity:** Private TypeScript "pseudonymous wealth-velocity" executive runtime — a multi-tempo orchestrator (millisecond to year) with wallet identity, file ledger, risk harness, signed PnL receipts, a Hyperliquid Phase-0 paper-track strategy, and a lottery layer (audit contests, hackathons, bounties).

**Frozen state:** built-never-deployed (no live URL, no package, no releases, private repo, no CI workflows).

**Shipped test:** FAIL — documented exec path (`npm ci && npm run orchestrator:dry`, README + package.json scripts) crashes at HEAD. Verified twice (auditor + adversarial verifier) in clean /tmp installs: `src/receipts/index.ts:2` imports ethers v6 named exports (`getAddress`, `verifyMessage`) while `package.json:32` pins `ethers 5.8.0` (v5 API) — `SyntaxError: The requested module 'ethers' does not provide an export named 'getAddress'` at boot of every CLI entry point. With `ethers@^6.13.0` the same path boots cleanly and 164/164 tests (12 files) pass.

**Evidence level:** runnable-from-repo (crash reproduced and fix verified by executing the repo in a disposable copy; no identity files touched, no secret values printed).

**Verdict:** PARK

**Minimal activation path:**
1. Mechanical fix: `package.json` line 32 — change `"ethers": "5.8.0"` to `"ethers": "^6.13.0"`, run `npm install` to regenerate `package-lock.json` (verified: cradle boots and 164/164 tests pass on v6).
2. `npm ci && npm run orchestrator:dry` → cradle boots paper-mode (`ENABLE_LIVE_TRADING=false`, `LEDGER_BACKEND=file`); `npm test` → 12 files / 164 tests green.
3. Operate Phase-0 paper-track: hyperliquid strategy (`src/strategies/direct/hyperliquid/`) via orchestrator; verify signed receipts with `npm run receipts:list` / `receipts:verify` (`src/receipts/cli.ts`).
4. Financial activation (CONDUCTOR DECISION — pending in conductor queue): set `ENABLE_LIVE_TRADING=true` plus secrets by name — `HYPERLIQUID_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ETH_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY`, off-ramp pair `COINBASE_API_KEY`/`COINBASE_API_SECRET` or `KRAKEN_API_KEY`/`KRAKEN_API_SECRET`, RPC via `ALCHEMY_API_KEY` or `INFURA_API_KEY`; bounty surfaces `HACKERONE_API_KEY`/`HUNTR_API_KEY` optional (two-gate design, `src/config/index.ts`).
5. Close seed.yaml drift: `seed.yaml:22` declares `.github/workflows/ci.yml` which does not exist locally or on GitHub (404) — adding CI would have caught the ethers regression (introduced by commit `352f0d5`, the 2026-05-22 npm-audit fix for #1).

**Reason for PARK:** Financial activation is an explicitly pending conductor decision (live-trading keys, KYC off-ramps, pseudonymous wallets) — not an agent call; and HEAD is runtime-broken by the ethers 5.8.0 pin until the mechanical fix lands.

**PR candidate (not opened this run):** `package.json` line 32: `"ethers": "5.8.0"` → `"ethers": "^6.13.0"`, then `npm install` to regenerate `package-lock.json`. Root cause: commit `352f0d5` (2026-05-22 npm-audit fix, closed issue #1) downgraded ethers to v5 while `src/receipts/index.ts`, `src/identity/wallet.ts`, and `src/identity/pseudonym.ts` use v6-only named exports (`getAddress`, `verifyMessage`, `keccak256`, `toUtf8Bytes`, `HDNodeWallet`). 1-line manifest change + lockfile regen, well under 30 lines.

**Notes:** Pushed 2026-05-29 (recent — stale-default rule not triggered). Local checkout lives at `.home-cartridge/Code/persona-fleet`, NOT `~/Workspace/meta-organvm/` — registry/path drift worth recording. `.persona-fleet/` holds two live Ethereum wallet identity files under a `CONSTITUTIONAL_DO_NOT_DELETE` guard — all run-path verification done in disposable /tmp copies (removed); no identity files touched. Monetization surface: direct trading PnL (Hyperliquid paper→live via two-gate config) plus lottery-layer contest/bounty submissions (`src/strategies/lottery/`: sherlock-scanner, audit-contests, hackathons, huntr, vercel-oss, anthropic-safety).

body:
**Identity:** Python CLI (`organvm-scrutator`) that scans workspace `.claude/plans` directories into a plan visibility index and is designed to track questions/suggestions/atoms/energy as a self-auditing governance instrument — the parked P4 Receipt-Dashboard/Done-Receipt lane candidate in the conductor's manifest.

**Frozen state:** deployed-never-used

**Shipped test:** PASS — documented path in repo `CLAUDE.md` works: fresh clone of origin/main → `python3 -m venv .venv && pip install -e .` (exit 0) → `organvm-scrutator scan` indexed 1143 real plans from `~/.claude/plans` + workspace (2026-06-11); `organvm-scrutator stats` exit 0 (verifier re-ran). Caveat: the deployed daily CI runs the same commands on a GitHub runner with no workspace and commits empty indices (`total_plans: 0` at origin/main HEAD, verified) daily, and the questions/suggestions/atoms/energy half reads `data/raw/*.jsonl` stores that nothing ever writes (`data/raw/` absent from HEAD, verified).

**Evidence level:** CI-confirmed (Daily Scan + Push on main green daily through 2026-06-11 via `gh run list` — but green is hollow: committed `data/indices/visibility-index.json` shows `total_plans: 0` and `data/research/gap-queue.yaml` shows `gaps: []` every day).

**Verdict: PARK**

**Minimal activation path:**
1. Park (one-line reason): the metrics core (question/suggestion/atom/energy collection hooks feeding `data/raw/*.jsonl`) was never built — `src/organvm_scrutator/governance/question_ledger.py` is a 354-byte stub, `stats` reports all-zero metrics with Metabolic State UNKNOWN — and the deployed daily CI (`.github/workflows/daily-scan.yml`) commits empty indices from a workspace-less runner; activation means building the collection pipeline, not flipping a switch. The conductor's manifest already parks it as the P4 Receipt-Dashboard lane.

**Reason for PARK:** Plausible internal value (plan-scanner half works today: 3 steps to a real 1143-plan index), but the governance-metrics core is unfed/unbuilt, the daily CI loop emits a false-active signal on empty data, and the conductor's manifest already parks it at P4 — activation not justified now. Not on PyPI (JSON API 404), no releases, README on main is 78 bytes (`CLAUDE.md` is the real doc); all commits since 2026-06-06 are bot commits ("chore: daily scrutator scan").

**Verifier corrections (adversarial pass, 2026-06-11):** auditor's risk note of "6 unpushed commits" at `/Users/4jp/Code/organvm/organvm-scrutator` is refuted — that checkout is on branch `claude/consolidate-enterprise-repos-3VVeS`, fully pushed to origin (0 unpushed vs its tracking branch, clean tree); it is unmerged-into-main feature work (6 ahead / 12 behind origin/main), not unpushed work. All other load-bearing claims independently confirmed. Verdict unchanged.

**PR candidate (not opened this run):** Two small fixes (text-only candidates, no workflow edits performed): (1) `.github/workflows/daily-scan.yml` commits empty data because `PlanScanner` defaults to `~/Workspace` + `~/.claude/plans`, which don't exist on GitHub runners — add a ~6-line guard step after 'Run scan' (skip commit when `jq .total_plans data/indices/visibility-index.json` == 0) or remove the cron trigger, ending the daily false-active commits of empty indices. (2) `src/organvm_scrutator/scanner/plan_scanner.py` `_scan_directory` treats subdirectories as files, producing 'Errno 21 Is a directory' warnings on `/Users/4jp/.claude/plans` and `organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/data` during stats — a 2-line `is_file()` guard fixes it.

Pointers stale on every submodule, internally labeled 'archived superproject' on its own PR branch, private with a 404 homepage — activation adds nothing the per-repo surfaces don't already provide. **Shipped test:** PASS — README-documented executable path verified end-to-end from a fresh shallow clone (/tmp/hm-audit): `python3 -m venv && pip install -e .` (Python 3.14, pydantic 2), then every README API example executed and independently re-executed by the verifier: `load_hierarchia('.')` → 42 strata / 676 modules; `h.search_modules('entropy')` → 24 (matches README); `get_registry('.').by_type(ModuleType.LAW)` → 106 (matches); `by_scale(Scale.COSMIC)` → 124 (matches); `validate_hierarchy('.')` → 0 errors, 60 warnings (README says 0/41 — drift, not breakage).

**Evidence level:** runnable-from-repo (full documented API executed twice from a fresh clone; all dead-link claims re-probed live this run).

**Verdict:** ship-now (auditor verdict upheld by adversarial verification; 3 months stale but direct execution overrides the staleness default).

**Minimal activation path:**
1. Fix dead-org links (probed 404 this run): README.md footer `github.com/ivviiviivvi` → `github.com/organvm-i-theoria` (200 confirmed); architecture link `github.com/ivviiviivvi/narratological-algorithmic-lenses` → `github.com/a-organvm/narratological-algorithmic-lenses` (old 404, new 200 confirmed); seed.yaml line `org: ivviiviivvi` → `org: organvm-i-theoria`.

body:
## IRF

IRF-OPS-093

## Problem

A hall-monitor closeout found that `organvm session export` can write durable-looking closeout markdown into a non-git workspace path. Example evidence from peer-audited--behavioral-blockchain: `/Users/4jp/Workspace/meta-organvm/praxis-perpetua/sessions/2026-06-07--peer-audited-local-dev-config-commit.md` existed under a path that is not a git repository.

That creates a persistence vacuum: the artifact looks saved locally but has no automatic local:remote=1:1 guarantee.

## Expected fix

Closeout/export tooling should detect non-git destinations and either refuse, mirror into a tracked repo, or auto-commit+push through the canonical session archive surface.

Pairs with IRF-DST-004, IRF-OPS-090, and IRF-SYS-243.
title: Fix session export persistence for non-git destinations

**Open client bug-report loop with no tracking surface until now.**

Maddie reported (2026-05-16) that clicking to deselect an answer "tried to send an email." The studio asked her to point at which page/quiz (docs/maddie/2026-05-16-outbound-implementation-confirmation.md:38,71); no response captured; the item silently dropped out of the 2026-05-25 and 2026-06-03 outbounds.

**Best hypothesis:** quiz auto-advance has no deselect affordance — a second click may have hit the result-panel mailto/capture CTA. Cannot confirm without her repro (page + what she clicked).

**Paths:** (a) get repro from Maddie (fold into next outbound), (b) preemptively add a deselect/back affordance to /quiz (UX hardening), or (c) confirm-and-close if unreproducible after (a).

body:
**Unreconciled drift between the locked architecture doc and shipped code, surfaced in the 2026-04-29 ideals-vs-rendered diff (D-005, W-041 PARTIAL) — no tracking surface until now.**

- Locked-arch doc: node 2 → Inner, node 12 → Identity
- src/data/hub.config.ts (shipped everywhere): nodes 1–5 → Physical, 6–8 → Inner, 9–11 → Identity, 12–13 → Financial (node 2 → Physical, node 12 → Financial)

The code mapping is live across the spiral, quiz scoring, node pages, and lineage surfaces. The pillar ORDER (Physical → Inner → Identity → Financial) is de-facto shipped; the per-node mapping discrepancy is the open question.

**Resolution paths:** (a) update the locked-arch doc to match shipped code (cheapest, codifies reality), (b) change hub.config.ts to match the doc (touches quiz scoring + 5 data layers — see CLAUDE.md Configuration Layering), or (c) put it to Maddie as a /decisions card.

Source: .claude/plans/2026-04-29-maddie-ideals-vs-rendered-diff.md (D-005/W-041). Found by the 2026-06-05 debt-clearance sweep (4jp-prompt-corpus reviewer).

body:
Currently, all 1,831 tests across the project are unit-level tests utilizing a mocked `Pool` for database interactions. 

The Triadic Review flagged this as a real gap. Without actual database integration tests, we risk regressions and false positives, as we just saw with the loss of `EXCLUDED.*` fields during ON CONFLICT operations on submitAttestation (PR #659).

We need an integration test suite that:
1. Provisions an ephemeral real PostgreSQL instance (via testcontainers or similar)
2. Runs the database migrations
3. Tests database-coupled services with actual queries rather than mocks

This must be addressed before re-running the Triadic Review.
title: Implement integration test suite for real database interactions

**Session receipt** — proof-of-work for session `00a83261-0d61-4ac0-9771-578494aec432` (Claude, home-scope `/Users/4jp`), 2026-05-30 12:46→13:03Z.

Computer shut down mid/post the prior `heartbeat-live-flip` session. This session re-oriented after restart, verified the prior session's claims against disk (memory-is-hypothesis), and drained all hanging work. Canonical ledger entry: **DONE-569** in `INST-INDEX-RERUM-FACIENDARUM.md`.

## What landed (every SHA)

| # | Work | Repo | Visibility | SHA / link |
|---|------|------|-----------|-----------|
| 1 | Merge PR #92 — clipboard NULL-timestamp fix (DONE-567) | a-organvm/organvm-engine | public | [`6ff4323`](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-engine/commit/6ff4323) · [PR #92](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-engine/pull/92) |
| 2 | Claim DONE-569 (counter lock) | a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm | public | [`179559e`](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/commit/179559e) |
| 3 | Log DONE-569 — 5-plan prune recovery + additive-mirror root-cause | a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm | public | [`53d0a44`](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/commit/53d0a44) |
| 4 | Fold portal-router orphan plans into provenance ledger | a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm | public | [`7cb7f55`](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/commit/7cb7f55) |
| 5 | Prompt-registry auto-capture (background churn, atomic) | a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm | public | [`7d7af9c`](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/commit/7d7af9c) |
| 6 | Heartbeat Jules digest (post-restart first fire) | 4444J99/session-meta | private | `e13a65a8` |
| 7 | Home-root scan regen | 4444J99/_limen | private | `9b26838` |
| 8 | Closeout receipt (chezmoi source) | 4444J99/domus-semper-palingenesis | private | `b5ae7ff` |

Prior-session recovery commits verified durable this session: `session-meta 3cad8fd7` (5-plan restore) + `domus bc0ad8d` (additive-mirror fix).

## Verification

`/verify` **PASS** — drove `organvm prompts clipboard --dry-run` against the merged main (editable install): exit 0, **1112 unique prompts / 406 sessions**, no `TypeError`. Probe confirmed the fix is load-bearing: exactly **1** NULL-`ZTIMESTAMP` row exists in the 12,883-row live DB — the documented single point of failure.

## Final state

- All 5 touched repos at **0/0** (clean, pushed).
- Session **archived** to `~/.claude/sessions/2026-05-30--computer-shut-down-last-session-before/` (copy-based; live transcript intact).
- Open residual: **IRF-OPS-080 (#375)** — a-i--skills `validate` CI debt (not this session's work).

## Notable

The DONE-569 fix (dropping `--delete` from the plans mirror) proved itself in production within hours: a concurrent session's plan rename removed a closeout from runtime + chezmoi source, but the now-additive session-meta mirror preserved its content — exactly the guarantee DONE-569 shipped.

body:
## Finding
The `validate` CI job is **red on `a-i--skills` main** (HEAD `1f43a2e`, still red post-merge of #20 at `1a36d81`). Confirmed pre-existing — not introduced by PR #20 (portal-router validates clean). Surfaced 2026-05-30 while verifying #20 mergeability.

## Why it didn't block the merge
`required_status_checks: null` on the `main` branch protection — `validate` is **not** a required check, so it does not gate merges. This is latent repo-hygiene debt, not a release blocker.

## Three offending skills
| Skill | Violation |
|---|---|
| `skills/tools/artifact-resurfacing` | `governance_norm_group: memory-hygiene` not in allowed enum |
| `skills/development/parametrize-and-cite` | description 996 chars (max 600) **and** `governance_norm_group: artifact-hygiene` invalid |
| `skills/tools/workspace-autopsy-governance` | missing `license` in frontmatter |

Allowed `governance_norm_group` enum: `distribution-readiness`, `documentation-standard`, `quality-gate`, `repo-hygiene`, `security-baseline`.

## Remediation (separate PR — do not bundle with feature work)
1. Remap `memory-hygiene` → `repo-hygiene` (or `quality-gate`) on artifact-resurfacing.
2. Remap `artifact-hygiene` → `repo-hygiene` and trim parametrize-and-cite description to ≤600 chars.
3. Add `license: MIT` to workspace-autopsy-governance.
4. Re-run `scripts/validate_skills.py --collection example/document --unique --check-links` until green.

## Adjacent (non-blocking) observation
`~/_arms/mirror/source` symlinks the a-i--skills **repo root** while sibling mirrors (`claude`/`codex`/`gemini`) point at **skills dirs** — causing an over-nested browse path (`mirror/source/skills/tools/<skill>`). Load path is unaffected (flat `~/.claude/skills`). Repoint candidate: `mirror/source → a-i--skills/skills`. Logged here for continuity; deferred per wrap-up scope.

Filed by: session 2026-05-30 (post #20 merge).
title: [IRF-OPS-080] a-i--skills validate job red on main — 3 skills with invalid frontmatter

body:
Tracking issue for the deployment-architecture & release-discipline evolution. Full plan: [`docs/logos/evolution-plan.md`](../blob/claude/issue-discovery-reporting-2R1GO/docs/logos/evolution-plan.md) (on PR #234's branch until merged).

### Why
`main` == production with no gate; releasing == merging; breaking-without-config changes become live incidents; infra is spread across **Vercel + Render + Terraform/AWS + k8s** (too much surface for a solo operator). Cloudflare account already runs 9 Workers / 3 D1 / 1 R2 — an underused strength.

### Decision needed
Platform direction. Recommended: **Cloudflare-hybrid now → full-Cloudflare later** (Pages for SPA + CF edge in front of a Render/Fly Node backend that keeps Express + BullMQ + Redis + Postgres; later strangler-migrate to Workers + Queues + Cron + Hyperdrive). Alternatives: consolidate on Render (A) or Railway (B). See plan §3.

### Release discipline (any option) — §4
- [ ] Merge ≠ release: `main` → **staging** auto; **prod = manual promote**
- [ ] Environments: local → PR preview → staging → prod
- [ ] Branch protection on `main`: full CI (typecheck + server tests + build) + review required
- [ ] Per-env secrets; feature-flag breaking changes; expand-contract migrations; Sentry + health checks

### Phases (sequences every open item)
- [ ] **P0 Guardrails** — branch protection, CI gates, staging target (`main`→staging)
- [ ] **P1 Safe-merge backlog** — #233, #229, #237; flag-gate + merge #234; tick #235 per-env
- [ ] **P2 Platform consolidation** — CF Pages + Render/Fly backend; resolve #230; retire terraform/ + k8s/
- [ ] **P3 Debt** — #236 (frontend TS), Sentry, test gates
- [ ] **P4 Meta** — #238 (IRF/omega/seed/concordance)
- [ ] **P5 (optional) Full Cloudflare** — strangler-migrate API→Workers, BullMQ→Queues/Cron, Redis→DO/Upstash, Postgres via Hyperdrive

Related: #234 #235 #230 #237 #236 #238 #233 #229
title: [EPIC] Deployment evolution: release discipline + platform consolidation (Cloudflare)

body:
`main` is live-deployed, and PR #234 is **breaking unless these are in place first**. This issue is the merge/deploy gate.

### Must be done before merging/deploying #234
- [ ] **Auth0: issue an `org_id` claim** (and optionally `tier`) on access tokens. The multi-tenant IDOR fix derives org from this claim; **without it, `/api/deals` and `/api/contacts` return 403 for every user** (fail-closed by design). Namespaced custom claims (`https://…/org_id`) are supported.
- [ ] **Set webhook secrets in prod**: `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, `SENDGRID_WEBHOOK_VERIFICATION_KEY`, `PLAID_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Verifiers now **fail closed (401)** when unset (previously fail-open).
- [ ] **Ensure `JWT_SECRET` is set in every environment** — `validateConfig()` now throws on boot if missing (insecure fallback removed).
- [ ] **Run the app under a non-owner DB role** so RLS (migration `018`) actually enforces — the table owner bypasses non-FORCEd policies. The per-request `app.current_org_id` GUC is wired via `orgContextMiddleware`.
- [ ] **Coordinate migrations `014`–`019` rollout** — `014` sets `prospects.org_id NOT NULL` and backfills existing rows to a default "Unassigned" org.
- [ ] **Confirm `organizations.subscription_tier` → data-tier mapping** matches product intent (dataTier entitlement now reads this column; the `x-data-tier` header is ignored).

Ref: PR #234, `docs/logos/praxis.md` §C.
title: Deploy prerequisites for PR #234 security hardening (breaking without config)

## Context

Cross-fleet onslaught synthesis (Claude Code session `704ff1f1` in `-Users-4jp-Code` scope, 2026-05-22). Surveyed 39 close-out plans from past ~36h, filtered to 18 candidates with explicit deferred-work signal, deduplicated into 25 numbered + 6 footnote clusters. Synthesis at chezmoi-source commit [`175c927`](https://github.com/4444J99/domus-semper-palingenesis/commit/175c927) (file `~/.claude/plans/2026-05-22-onslaught-collected-sibling-sessions.md`).

IRF rows landed at corpvs commit [`046a57f`](https://github.com/a-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/commit/046a57f) (INST-INDEX-RERUM-FACIENDARUM.md lines 2414+).

This issue tracks external propagation per corpvs CLAUDE.md requirement: *"REQUIRED: propagate completions to ALL interested external indices — close GitHub issues."*

## IRF rows (check when closed)

### Operational (OPS) — agent-executable or batch
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-070** (P3) — Cluster C8: stale `.git/rebase-merge/` dir in chezmoi-source verified false-positive of dir-presence heuristic; `rm -rf` awaits user ack, heuristic refinement ships independently
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-071** (P1) — Cluster C9: INDEX.md regenerator hook committed-not-deployed; one `chezmoi apply` closes the 940→486 plan-count drift
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-072** (P3) — Cluster C12: 11 stale worktree branches in `_dot-config`; user `p) prune` hotkey nod (tracked in `4444J99/_dot-config#1`)
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-073** (P2) — Cluster C15: 4 corpvs branches without upstream; per-branch user push-or-delete
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-074** (P2) — Cluster C17: cross-system store classification (Codex/Gemini/OpenCode/Cursor); gated on IRF-OPS-077
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-075** (P2) — Cluster C18: 161 distributions audit completion (non-Claude trees); Codex dispatch
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-076** (P4) — Cluster C19: `/Users/4jp/file.txt` 199KB atuin preview orphan; single-file Trash
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-077** (P1) — Cluster C1: canonical-shape model decision (tier-by-kind A/B/C vs capability-tagged); blocks Phase 3/4 of skills marketplace
- [ ] **IRF-OPS-078** (P3) — Cluster C20: chezmoi script rename propagation; `chezmoi status` to disambiguate genuine-rename vs normal-tracking

### Constitutional (CRP) — user-gated drift

- [ ] **IRF-CRP-016** (P1) — Cluster C5: `/Users/4jp/CLAUDE.md` line ~35 IRF stats drift (header says "957 items, 21 domains"; actual 866 rows / 47 prefixes)

## Note on cluster C10

Cluster C10 (Edit→chezmoi-add hook deployment) was **subsumed by existing IRF-OPS-068** rather than filed as a new row. Same root cause class; closure of OPS-068 closes C10.

## Vulnerability Report

`npm audit` found 4 vulnerabilities (2 moderate, 2 high) requiring `--force`:

### drizzle-orm (high)
SQL injection via improperly escaped SQL identifiers
- **Fix:** `drizzle-orm@0.45.2` (outside stated range)
- GHSA-gpj5-g38j-94v9

### next (high)
13 advisories: DoS, cache poisoning, XSS, SSRF, middleware bypass
- **Fix:** `next@16.2.6` (outside stated range)

### postcss (moderate)
XSS via unescaped `</style>` in CSS Stringify Output
- GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93

### yaml (moderate)
Stack overflow via deeply nested YAML collections
- **Fix:** `yaml@2.9.0` (outside stated range)
- GHSA-48c2-rrv3-qjmp

**Action:** Run `npm audit fix --force` to resolve.

body:
## Problem

Recent planning docs in \`~/.claude/plans/\` use generic letter labels (\`A\`, \`B\`, \`C\`) for plan branches when descriptive names would carry more meaning and survive context loss better.

Example from the canonical domain stand-up plan:
- \`A\` = market activation
- \`B\` = IRF propagation
- \`C\` = audit-engine extraction

Letter labels require the reader to look up the mapping every time; descriptive names are self-documenting.

## Closure options

- **(a)** Audit \`~/.claude/plans/\` for A/B/C-labeled plans, propose descriptive renames, archive originals (~45 min)
- **(b)** Add CLAUDE.md guidance under "Plan File Discipline" prohibiting generic letter labels going forward (~10 min, documentation-only)
- **(c)** Leave existing plans, enforce naming discipline for new plans (~5 min)

## Acceptance

New plans use descriptive branch names; existing A/B/C-labeled plans either renamed or grandfathered with a deprecation note.

## Notes

The v4 of the canonical domain stand-up plan already uses descriptive sequencing alongside the A/B/C labels — every letter is mapped to a phase name in the plan body. Treat this issue as making that pattern the rule, not the exception.

Internal cross-reference: IRF-III-051.
title: Replace A/B/C plan branch labels with descriptive names

body:
## Problem

`.env.example` now declares 8 brand bindings as the canonical authority for project identity (commit `4239476`, Project Identity section):

- `CANONICAL_DOMAIN`, `PROJECT_NAME`, `MOBILE_BUNDLE_ID`, `DEEP_LINK_SCHEME`
- `CONTACT_EMAIL_DOMAIN`, `RENDER_API_SERVICE_NAME`, `RENDER_WEB_SERVICE_NAME`, `CANONICAL_REPO_URL`

The same values are also duplicated by hand across:

- `apps/mobile/ios/Info.plist`
- `apps/mobile/android/build.gradle`
- `render.yaml`
- `package.json` files
- `docker-compose.yml`
- ngrok config yaml
- `legal/*.md`, `outreach/*.md`, `docs/architecture/*.md`

Each duplication is a drift risk. Propagation discipline is currently convention-only (a NOTE block in `.env.example` documents which files to update in lockstep).

## Closure options

- **(a)** Pre-commit hook diffs `.env.example` brand values against propagation targets, fails on mismatch (~60 min including tests)
- **(b)** Generator script materializes platform-specific files from `.env` at build time via \`envsubst\` or \`mustache\` (~90 min, more invasive but durable)
- **(c)** Audit-only — document the propagation map in \`docs/architecture/brand-propagation-map.md\`, no enforcement (~30 min)

## Acceptance

Changing a brand value in `.env.example` either (1) propagates automatically to all targets at build time, or (2) fails CI loudly with a list of out-of-sync files.

## Context

Discovered during a root-layer pass on the canonical domain stand-up plan (\`docs/planning/planning--styx-sequence-b-a-c--canonical-domain-standup--2026-05-17.md\`). Pre-blocker to plan Phase 2 (propagation hardening).

Internal cross-reference: IRF-III-049.
title: Propagate brand identity from .env to platform-specific files at build time

## IRF-SYS-184: Plugin Ecosystem Design

**Priority:** P1
**Source:** S-2026-05-17-knowledge-base-epistemic-export

### Description
Designed 4 priority meta-plugins for session orchestration:

1. **session-orchestrator** — Master sequencer for Phase 0→3 chain (DIWS substrate → Diagnosis → Start → Insert Rack → Close-Out → Master Bus)
2. **vacuum-radar** — Real-time N/A detection during session (N/A = vacuum rule)
3. **triple-reference-tracker** — Enforce IRF+repo+GH issue during work (Triple Reference axiom)
4. **atom-logger** — Log work units to prompt registry

### Architecture
- **3-layer design:** Meta-Plugins (4 new), Existing Skills (~160 mapped), New Skills (gap-fillers)
- **Gain staging rules** defined to prevent context clipping and maintain truth fidelity
- **Implementation pending** — design complete, build not started

### Next Steps
- Build session-orchestrator (master sequencer)
- Build vacuum-radar (real-time N/A detection)
- Build triple-reference-tracker (enforce triple reference)
- Build atom-logger (log to prompt registry)
title: [IRF-SYS-184] Plugin ecosystem design — 4 priority meta-plugins for session orchestration

Premortem on the plan to "extract smallest sellable artifact & get external payment within 30 days" — assuming it already failed 6 months from now.

**20 failure modes identified:**
1. Plan becomes artifact (extraction plan generates extraction plans)
2. Nothing small to extract (system is monolithic)
3. 1,107 tests worthless as collateral (internal validation ≠ market value)
4. Peer auditing requires peers (bootstrap paradox)
5. Linguistic cloaker schizophrenia (gambling terms swapped in production)
6. Governance file leakage (seed.yaml, AGENTS.md expose internal structure)
7. No sales pipeline (zero external contacts)
8. Previous GTM unexecuted (March 2026 plans died same way)
9. Identity inseparable from ORGANVM (can't sell without ecosystem)
10. Inherited bugs (519 open issues)
... and 10 more

**Hidden assumption:** Internal completeness (tests, governance, beta-ready) = market value

**Revised plan:**
- Week 1: 20 cold messages to compliance/audit/fintech roles
- Week 2: 15-min calls, listen to problems
- Week 3: Extract minimum for one specific problem
- Week 4: Send invoice

**Pre-launch checklist:**
- Plan-to-action ratio < 3:1
- "Explain without ORGANVM" test
- Hard stop on internal commits until 5 external messages sent

**Artifacts:** premortem-report-20260517-144746.html, premortem-transcript-20260517-144746.md

**Commit:** 85d1ded
title: premortem: extract sellable artifact + get paid in 30 days

Six-lens expansive inquiry on the Styx revenue gap: why a system with 1,107 passing tests, GRADUATED governance, and beta-ready status has zero external users and zero revenue.

**Lenses executed:**
- **Scope:** Framed the inquiry around scaling, App Store compliance, and the "why no money" paradox
- **Logic:** Viable revenue path is B2B SaaS extraction of the audit framework
- **Mythos:** System is "complete at governance, not market" — validation loop is internal
- **Bridge:** Three strategic forks identified: Parasitic Embedding, B2B SaaS Extraction, One External Sale
- **Meta:** Epistemic signature: logic-heavy + metaphorically-rich + bridge-actionable + self-referential + decision-poor
- **Pattern:** Core topology is Möbius strip of self-validation

**Synthesis:** System must shift validation from "tests pass" to "external payment"

**Artifacts:** expansive-inquiry--styx-revenue-gap/ (7 files)

**Commit:** 85d1ded
title: expansive inquiry: Styx revenue gap — why no money

body:
## Finding

During the 2026-05-17 Styx hall-monitor closeout, the required session closeout command failed:

    organvm prompts distill --dry-run

It exited with:

    Input file not found: /Users/4jp/Code/organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm/data/atoms/clipboard-prompts.json
    Run organvm prompts clipboard first to generate clipboard prompts.

## Why this is a vacuum

Repo auto-generated AGENTS and CLAUDE instructions tell sessions to run organvm prompts distill --dry-run at closeout, but the default command has an implicit precondition that is not represented in the closeout protocol. A hall-monitor closeout can therefore report a blocked distillation pass without a first-class tracked work item.

## Recommended closure

Pick one:

1. Change the closeout instruction to run organvm prompts clipboard before organvm prompts distill --dry-run when the default input is absent.
2. Teach organvm prompts distill --dry-run to auto-generate or gracefully explain the missing default input with a machine-readable status.
3. Point closeout docs at an explicit existing input path when session prompts have already been exported.

## Evidence

Observed from /Users/4jp/Code/organvm/peer-audited--behavioral-blockchain on 2026-05-17 during closeout verification.
title: organvm prompts distill default closeout command requires missing clipboard-prompts precondition

body:
## IRF-RES-012

**Wave**: 5 | **TE**: 250K | **Motor**: boundary-objects | **Owner**: Agent (ARCHITECTURE)

### Source citation

SYN-03 §5.1 (4 BO structural types); RP-05 §4.3, §5.2, §7.1 (immutable mobiles + per-actant enforcement); SYN-02 §4.3.

### Deliverable

Redesign three artifacts using three different Star & Griesemer BO structural types: `seed.yaml` (Repository — per-community fields), `CLAUDE.md` template (Standardized form — interpretive-community headers), `governance-rules.json` (Ideal type — typed schemas with local-adaptation slots). Each artifact must inscribe three enforcement paths visibly: human (advisory), AI (behavioral constraint), CI/CD (compliance check). Plus `interpretation_paths` per actant + `decision_gates` (obligatory passage points) + re-enrollment notification layer.

### Hard dependencies

RES-006 (paired — vocab teaches the BO repository pattern); RES-007 (visibility schema).

### Success criterion

Three artifacts redesigned and migrated across all 145 repos; per-actant enforcement paths visible in each; OPP transitions documented; re-enrollment cycle established.

### Notes from corpus grounding

**Heaviest item** — touches every repo. Per RP-05 §5.2: 'A CLAUDE.md that reads identically to human, AI, and CI/CD is a failed immutable mobile.' Per RP-05 §7.3: enrollment contracts must be re-read as context changes, requiring subscription/notification. The three different BO types map cleanly to the three artifacts (per SYN-03 §5.1) — not one pattern repeated, but three distinct patterns.

---

## IRF-RES-010

**Wave**: 5 | **TE**: 130K | **Motor**: tarskian | **Owner**: Agent (ARCHITECTURE)

### Source citation

RP-02 §5.7, §6.4 (Löb-theorem application).

### Deliverable

Architectural separation of two operational modes: inner-loop maintenance vs. outer-loop improvement. Tooling enforcement of the boundary. Documentation classifying each governance operation as maintenance-allowed vs. improvement-only.

### Hard dependencies

RES-007 (need visibility to distinguish improvement from maintenance); RES-008 (IRA charter constrains how meta-governance can safely operate).

### Success criterion

Two distinct operational modes documented and tooling-enforced; every governance operation classified; the system cannot simultaneously maintain its operational governance and redesign that governance.

### Notes from corpus grounding

Per RP-02 §6: 'meta-governance loop' is a Löb-theorem impossibility wall. Temporal staging (RES-013) operationalizes the separation by creating distinct waves; this RES item adds the *architectural* enforcement that makes the staging robust.

## IRF-RES-008

**Wave**: 4 | **TE**: 120K | **Motor**: tarskian | **Owner**: Human + Agent (GOVERNANCE)

### Source citation

SYN-02 §4.5, §5.3 (Tarskian escape via IRA); RP-02 §6.3.

### Deliverable

IRA panel charter + appeal/override protocol; first calibration run with multi-rater (≥3 raters, **must include external participants**); decision framework for routing semantic verdicts to IRA when automated checks declare incompleteness (per RES-007).

### Hard dependencies

RES-007 (incompleteness must be visible before delegation); RES-010 (charter constrains meta-governance separation).

### Success criterion

Functional IRA panel with documented charter, multi-rater calibration ≥3 raters, ≥1 external participant; appeal mechanism documented; first cycle of semantic verdicts processed.

### Notes from corpus grounding

Per SYN-03 §5.4 + SYN-02: single-operator IRA cannot escape Tarski's diagonal — the panel must include raters outside ORGANVM to function as the Tarskian truth-oracle. **External recruitment + commissioning is non-negotiable** and adds wallclock floor beyond the panel-convening 1–2 weeks.

---

## IRF-RES-007

**Wave**: 3 | **TE**: 90K | **Motor**: tarskian | **Owner**: Agent (GOVERNANCE)

### Source citation

RP-04 §3.1 (5 failure modes); SYN-02 §5.5 R1; RP-02 §5.5.

### Deliverable

Schema + retrofit such that every governance verdict emitter declares, for each of the 5 failure modes (reference / collision / drift / mismatch / governance), whether the verdict covers it. Surface the 5-tuple in verdict output.

### Hard dependencies

RES-006 (governance-failure-mode requires vocabulary to know what's in scope).

### Success criterion

Every verdict in `organvm-engine` returns a 5-tuple completeness declaration; consumers can route uncovered dimensions to IRA panel (RES-008's domain).

### Notes from corpus grounding

Per RP-04 §3.1 the 5 failure modes are: reference failure (name → nothing), namespace collision (name → multiple), semantic drift (meaning changed while name persisted), abstraction mismatch (same entity, different names per context), governance failure (no authoritative dispute resolution). This operationalizes the Tarskian escape that RES-008 formalizes — gives it concrete input shape.

---

**Wave**: 2 | **TE**: 130K | **Motor**: tarskian | **Owner**: Agent (GOVERNANCE)

### Source citation

SYN-02 §4.1, §5.2 (temporal staging); RP-02 §5.1, §6.1 (Löb-theorem application + staged self-reference).

### Deliverable

Refactor every governance validator so it compares current↔previous state, never current↔current. Includes staged-measurement protocol enforcing Goodhart-Campbell mathematical necessity (no continuous feedback from outcome measurement to governance).

### Hard dependencies

None — parallel-independent.

### Success criterion

All governance validators in `organvm-engine` have been audited and any current↔current self-validation is removed; staging boundaries are tooling-enforced; verdicts emitted with temporal-staging metadata.

### Notes from corpus grounding

Per SYN-02 §3 + RP-07 §7.3 (Strathern's audit-culture work): when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure. Continuous feedback loops between outcome measurement and governance are *mathematically forbidden*, not just stylistically discouraged. Touches every governance validator — broad surface area.

---

## IRF-RES-011

**Wave**: 2 | **TE**: 80K | **Motor**: boundary-objects | **Owner**: Agent (ARCHITECTURE)

### Source citation

RP-03 §3.4–3.5, §6.1–6.4 (coupling structure); SYN-02 §4.2.

### Deliverable

Codify in `governance-rules.json` the hybrid topology principle: inter-organ hierarchical (I→II→III, no back-edges) + intra-organ rhizomatic (shared CLAUDE.md, common stack). Add effective-vs-nominal coupling tracking dimension (RP-03 §4.5 scaling-limit finding).

### Hard dependencies

None — parallel-independent.

### Success criterion

`governance-rules.json` formally encodes the topology principle; `organvm governance audit` enforces it; effective-coupling metric computed periodically and reported alongside nominal coupling.

### Notes from corpus grounding

Per RP-03 §4.6, ORGANVM already instantiates the principle; this is *codification of an observed invariant*, not novel imposition. Hence downward TE pressure from 90K → 80K (codification simplicity offset by +10K for effective-coupling tracking). RP-03 §4.5 surfaces a scaling-limit: as organ count grows, loose inter-module coupling approaches zero information flow.

## IRF-RES-004

**Wave**: 1 | **TE**: 200K | **Motor**: measurement | **Owner**: Agent (MEASUREMENT)

### Source citation

RP-07 §6.2–6.3 (factor analysis); SYN-02 §5.5 R2; SYN-04 §3.2 (factor stability + relational psychometrics).

### Deliverable

Bayesian EFA on 17 omega items × 145 repos; factor structure report with ±0.15 confidence intervals; DIF (differential item functioning) analysis across Organ-I (26 repos) vs Organ-III (32 repos) subsamples; recommendation for RES-014 (proceed if multi-factor + cross-organ DIF, else close).

### Hard dependencies

None — independent of RES-006 but moved into Wave 1 per SYN-04 'data reveals what is measurable; governance ideology should follow'.

### Success criterion

Factor structure either confirmed unidimensional (and validated as such) OR mapped as multi-factor with documented cross-organ DIF; actionable input to RES-014 conditional-execution decision; ±0.15 CIs honestly reported.

### Notes from corpus grounding

Sample-size risk: 145 repos is at the lower margin of EFA viability (RP-07 §6.3 specifies 5–10 obs per item × 17 items = 85–170 minimum). Bayesian alternative (vs frequentist) provides smaller-sample tolerance +80K TE. If EFA fails to converge or factors are unstable, the entire measurement-validity motor cannot deliver as specified — this is a real commission-level risk, not a refinement.

---

- **Tracked in commission**: [INQ-2026-013 RES Bundle Commission RFC](https://github.com/meta-organvm/praxis-perpetua/blob/main/commissions/2026-05-09-RES-bundle-commission.md)

## IRF-RES-006

**Wave**: 1 | **TE**: 110K | **Motor**: boundary-objects | **Owner**: Agent (NAMING)

### Source citation

RP-04 §5.1 Principle 4 (synonymy catastrophe at ~50–100 naming decisions); SYN-03 §5.1 (controlled vocab as repository-type boundary object).

### Deliverable

Machine-readable JSON/YAML canonical→synonym map + CI hook validating new names. Initial canonical extraction across `registry-v2.json` (~145 repo names), `governance-rules.json` (verdict/state names), all `seed.yaml` files (organ names, edge types), IRF domain codes (21), `concordance.md` invocation namespaces (~7 namespaces, 100+ IDs), ontologia entity types. Synonymy reconciliation pass + drift report.

### Hard dependencies

None — bedrock.

### Success criterion

All governance artifacts cite the registry; CI hook blocks new names not in the registry (or flags them for governance review); synonymy reconciliation report identifies all near-synonym clusters in existing corpus.

### Notes from corpus grounding

Per RP-04 §5.1 Principle 4, the synonymy catastrophe begins at ~50–100 independent naming decisions. ORGANVM is in the low thousands. RES-006 must canonicalize *existing* usage, not just gate future names — it's retroactive reconciliation, not preventive infrastructure. Per SYN-03 §5.1, this IS the first boundary-object construction in the bundle (repository type), pairing it intellectually with RES-012's later redesign.

---

body:
**Source:** Maddie 2026-05-01 — "we can simplify it right they can just say here's my email. I want to reach out with a personalized filter plan for me... so that I can take your email go to take their address go to EWG put it in and then work with chat and ideally, I'm gonna build my own knowledge."
**Atomized-wants:** W-070.

## Current state
`HydrationNode.astro:465-471` already does ZIP → EWG → filter recommendation in two steps with email gate (`source=hydration-node`). The "simplification" is a *different* code path: email + address capture → Maddie does manual lookup → personalized plan back to user.

## Plan
Add a new capture form with `source=water-personalized-plan` + optional `address` field on `CapturePayload`. New CTA on `/water/` ("Send me a personalized filter plan"). Wire to existing capture pipeline (`src/pages/capture.ts`). No new sink — multiple `Promise.all` branches handle email→GHL forwarding.

## Depends on
W-069 stabilization (both paths share the EWG layer; clarity on which is the canonical "real EWG vs email-fallback" UX).
title: [water] W-070: email-simplified personalized filter plan path

body:
**Source:** Maddie 2026-05-01 — "I have a whole bottle of water section cause I have a bunch of information on this smart water [Voss?] water you could be drinking. They have the best marketing, but it is the most acidic and dehydrating... they put sodium in their water."
**Atomized-wants:** W-068.

## Current state
No bottled-water brand-level content in `src/content/branches/`, `nodes/`, or `pillars/`. Closest existing: `HydrationNode.astro:182-190` "Brita reality check" callout (already mentions fluoride gap, same axis Maddie cares about).

## Plan (under universal mandate M1 — multi-citation)
New content slot `src/content/branches/bottled-truth.md` (or similar). Per-brand mini-cards: brand → marketing claim → actual pH → sodium content → cost per gallon. Brands seed: Smart Water, Voss, Fiji, Aquafina, Dasani, Essentia, Liquid Death.

**Every assertion requires ≥2 independent citations** (brand product page + independent lab/regulatory/peer-reviewed source). Drafts ship with `status: draft-pending-citations` until both lands.

## Blocked on
Dual-citation sourcing per brand-claim. Maddie has offered to send "her notes" — fold in when received.
title: [content] W-068: bottled-water brand education section (M1 dual-citation gated)

body:
**Source:** Maddie 2026-05-01 — "I do not think the prices of the water or the bottles water pricing is right... if I need to go into the store myself."
**Atomized-wants:** W-067.

## Current state
Three hardcoded `BottledWaterCost` records at `src/data/hydration.config.ts:297-325` (Safeway $1.29, Fiji $2.49, Essentia $2.79). Monthly/yearly numbers derive from a hidden ~36 bottles/month assumption. No source citation, no last-verified stamp.

## Plan (under universal mandate M2 — no hardcoded dynamic data)
1. **Schema:** add `source`, `sourceCheckedAt`, `unitVolumeOz`, `monthlyVolumeAssumption` fields to `BottledWaterCost` interface. Make per-month / per-year math derivable, not stored.
2. **Externalize:** move BottledWaterCost array out of `hydration.config.ts` into `src/data/runtime/bottled-prices.json` (or KV namespace). Also captures `FilterTier.priceRange` + `affiliateUrl` (W-013).

## Blocked on
Maddie's in-store visit for real numbers (per-bottle, per-case, bottle size). Schema rework can land independently as a no-op data refactor.
title: [water] W-067: bottle pricing accuracy + externalize from source (M2)

body:
**Source:** Maddie 2026-05-01 voice memo — "the biggest issue with my ZIP Code is fluoride and I didn't see that pop-up. Fluoride is a very specific filter that you have to get a specific filter for so a lot of people spend a shit ton of money on the wrong filters and they don't even filter out what they want to filter."
**Atomized-wants:** W-069.
**Diagnosis:** `docs/decisions/2026-05-01-water-report-fluoride-bug-diagnosis.md`.

## Three live hypotheses
1. **H1 — Astro `_worker.js` shadows `functions/api/water-report.ts`** → 404 → `response.json()` throws → catch-block fallback fires (now includes fluoride post-`c778f48`).
2. **H2 — EWG HTML structure changed, parser silent fails** → server demo fallback fires (DEMO_REPORT contains fluoride; if true, Maddie would have seen it). Partially exonerated.
3. **H3 — EWG genuinely has no fluoride row for Maddie's utility** → UI gives no "checked but not detected" disclaimer. Fix is UI-only.

## Discriminator
Maddie's badge state: "Sample Data" → H1/H2; "ZIP {N}" → H3.

## Defensive ship (already live)
- `c778f48` adds Fluoride to client fallback list AND introduces 3-state `setDataSourceState()` banner (live / demo-server / demo-fallback). Every visitor now sees the discriminator surfaced.

## Held
- Route migration `functions/api/water-report.ts` → `src/pages/api/water-report.ts` (mirrors `src/pages/capture.ts` precedent). Ships once H1 is confirmed.
- "Common contaminants checked but not detected" sub-list. Ships if H3.

## Closes when
Maddie's badge state + ZIP arrive → reproduce → ship the discriminated fix → verify fluoride appears or is explicitly disclaimed-absent.
title: [bug] W-069: fluoride miss in EWG result (P0)

**Atomized-wants:** W-066 (`docs/client-decisions/2026-04-17-atomized-wants.md`).

## Status
- **A1 SHIPPED** — `251f698` lowered hybrid particle opacity 0.3 → 0.15 to let the existing 13 distinct sacred shapes read more cleanly. Live; awaits Maddie visual approval.
- A2 (default flip to `'visible'`) HELD pending A1 review.
- A3 (wire sacred-geometry-primitives renderer for unique procedural mesh per node) HELD pending A2.

Maddie visually approves the rendered state. Reversible via `?vessel=` querystring.
title: [spiral] W-066: nodes easily identifiable / enough differential

3. **Rotate any real secrets** that were detected
4. **Remove secrets from git history** using tools like BFG Repo-Cleaner or git-filter-repo
5. **Update affected videos** if secrets appear in walkthrough recordings

### Resources

- [GitHub Secret Scanning Documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/secret-scanning)

- [BFG Repo-Cleaner](https://rtyley.github.io/bfg-repo-cleaner/)

## Summary

The vacuum detector algorithm (Python implementation in `phase-3_research-atlas/vacuum-detector/algorithm.py`) is a governance-critical component with no test coverage. It operationalizes the constitutional law: *"If there's no data or statistics to prove whichever answer is the best one, then it's an opportunity for studying and figuring it out."*

Testing is the primary "shatter point" identified in the B+ evaluation grade — without tests, the algorithm cannot prove its accuracy.

## Requirements

- Write unit tests for VacuumDetector class
- Test detection of each vacuum type (structural, measurement, inference, attribution)
- Test priority ranking
- Test edge cases (empty evidence base, full coverage, etc.)
- Achieve test coverage > 80%

## Priority

**P0** — Primary shatter point, governance-critical
title: Add test suite to vacuum detector algorithm

- All 8 organs as nodes (with Greek names)

## Description

The \`organvm seed validate\` command checks seed.yaml files for schema compliance but doesn't warn about repos that declare zero produces/consumes edges. Per LEX-IV (Metabolism), every living formation must metabolize — consume input and produce output.

## Task

Add a warning-level check to seed validation that flags repos with empty \`produces: []\` AND empty \`consumes: []\` (both empty = metabolic failure signal).

## Implementation hints

- Validation logic: \`src/organvm_engine/seed/validate.py\` (or similar)
- This should be a WARNING, not an error (some repos legitimately have no edges yet)
- Output format: consistent with existing validation messages

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] \`organvm seed validate\` warns on repos with both empty produces and consumes
- [ ] Warning message references LEX-IV (Metabolism)
- [ ] Repos with at least one edge in either direction pass without warning
- [ ] Test added for both cases (empty edges, non-empty edges)

## Estimated effort

~20 minutes. Small, focused change to existing validation logic.

## Summary

Build `organvm_engine/corpus/` module — a computational bridge between the post-flood constitutional corpus (225 files) and praxis-perpetua operational corpus (2,728 files).

## IRF Reference

**IRF-SYS-104** — Corpus Knowledge Graph

## Background

Cross-reference analysis (S-corpus-graph, 2026-04-07/08) revealed:
- **Strong connections:** AMMOI, Alpha-Omega, Formation Protocol, SPEC notation
- **Gaps:** SVSE under-referenced, Faculty/Commission/Defense governance minimally cross-linked
- **No computational cross-corpus index exists** — concepts are the connective tissue but have no graph representation

## Scope

- 4-phase extraction pipeline (structural scan → concept enumeration → implementation links → SPEC cross-refs)

## Finding

**Source:** S-empirical 2026-04-07, user correction to Exercise 10 results
**IRF:** IRF-SYS-100

The empirical test suite (Ex10) classified all non-code files as "ceremony" (overhead). User corrected: in a system where repos are theses reaching toward ideal forms, many non-code files ARE the most important files — they're specifications for future work, not waste.

## Required 3-way classification

| Category | Definition | Detection |
|----------|-----------|-----------|
| **Code** | Executes now | `.py`, `.ts`, `.scd`, etc. |
| **Specification** | Declares intent for future execution | Unique `seed.yaml`, unique `CLAUDE.md` with architecture, README describing what repo WILL become |
| **True ceremony** | Serves no function beyond compliance | Duplicate `LICENSE`, copy-pasted `CONTRIBUTING.md`, identical boilerplate across repos |

**Test:** If a non-code file is unique to its repo (per Ex12 hash check), it's specification. If duplicated identically across many repos, it's true ceremony.

## Impact

- Exercise 10 code:ceremony ratio needs recalculation with 3-way split
- Repos previously scored as "ceremony-heavy" or "zero-code" may actually be specification-heavy (aspirational, with future work declared)
- Birth inventory (DONE-336) classified 2,834 "unclassified" docs — many are likely specifications

## Evidence

- Memory: `feedback_ceremony_is_specification.md` (organvm-iv-taxis project memory)
- Duplication data: `sovereign--ground/structure-tests/results/ex12_*.json`

## Labels

`irf`, `P2-GROWTH`, `system`
title: Ceremony-as-specification: 3-way classification needed for non-code measurement

**IRF:** IRF-SYS-099

The sovereign--ground analytical frame produced a classification of 15 ideal functional forms that the organism reaches toward. These are attractors, not static categories — the system's repos orbit these forms rather than instantiate them.

Modular synthesis mapping to a-organvm mechanisms was honestly assessed: 4/7 mappings are real structural correspondences, 3/7 are forced (imposed vocabulary rather than discovered structure). The oscillation thesis: the system doesn't converge to a fixed state but oscillates between form and material.

## Relation to other findings

- The 6 mereological categories in sovereign--ground (holds, cuts, joins, bounds, lacks, moves) map to 6 of 16 a-organvm mechanisms
- The Rosetta Stone finding maps 3 orthogonal classification systems (Registry functional_class, Gemini node_class, a-organvm mechanism)

body:
## Finding

**Source:** S-empirical 2026-04-06/07, `sovereign--ground/structure-tests/suite.py`
**IRF:** IRF-SYS-098

Six-exercise bottom-up measurement of ~/Workspace/ (106 repos × 10 features) reveals the 8-organ model is structurally arbitrary:

1. **Boundary Misalignment (Ex14):** Every organ boundary scores 1.0000 misalignment. Repos inside an organ are NO more similar to each other than to repos outside.
2. **Import Graph (Ex18):** 26 edges, 76% isolates, one hub (organvm-engine). Pure DAG, zero cycles.
3. **Co-change (Ex06):** 85% of co-change is cross-organ. Real work clusters: core engine (META+IV), personal output (4444J99), outer ring (V+VI+VII).
4. **Clustering (Ex01):** 5 groups emerge by code ratio — organs don't predict clusters.
5. **Depth↔Complexity (Ex17):** Correlation 0.0092. Nesting depth has no relationship to file complexity.
6. **Flatten (Ex21):** 81% of filenames already unique; collisions are packaging boilerplate only.

## Implication

The 8 organs function as filing cabinets (organizational taxonomy), not functional units (structural coupling). Any restructuring proposal should be tested against these empirical baselines rather than theoretical models.

## Evidence

- Test suite: `sovereign--ground/structure-tests/suite.py`
- Results: `sovereign--ground/structure-tests/results/*.json`
- Plans: `~/.claude/plans/2026-04-07-empirical-structure-organvm.md`

## Labels

`irf`, `P2-GROWTH`, `system`
title: Empirical audit: organ boundaries carry zero structural load

## Problem

Governance predicate 3 (EFFECT) requires every entity to produce ≥1 traceable effect on ≥1 other entity. Currently, `seed.yaml` declares produces/consumes edges but no validator checks whether entities actually produce their declared effects.

An entity can claim to produce signals it never emits. Orphan existence is undetectable.

## Required

- `validate_effect_obligation` engine validator
- Check declared produces edges against actual outputs
- Wire into `organvm governance audit --effect-obligation`

- IRF-SYS-081 / #304

## Problem

All 6 existing governance predicates (IDENTITY through FINITUDE) are implemented only at organ-to-organ scale via `dependency_graph.py`, `governance-rules.json`, and schema-definitions. None operates at entity, directory, or function level.

The base genome must work "from the most bottom primitive to the top of the system."

## Required

- Predicate enforcement at directory level
- Predicate enforcement at entity level
- Same governance logic at every hierarchical level, not just organ-to-organ

## Parent

- IRF-SYS-081 / #304

## Study

`praxis-perpetua/studies/governance-genome-predicates-2026-04-05.md`
title: IRF-SYS-083: Scale propagation — governance predicates operate at organ level only

body:
## Problem

Governance predicate 7 (IMMUTABILITY) is entirely absent from the system. `governance-rules.json` is freely mutable with no amendment history, no append-only constraint, no constitutional protection.

**Critical:** Its absence means all other governance predicates can be silently invalidated by editing the governance file.

## Required

- Amendment log mechanism for governance-rules.json
- Write-once base definitions (axioms cannot be modified, only appended)
- Versioned governance history (every change recorded, none deleted)

## Parent

- IRF-SYS-081 / #304

## Study

`praxis-perpetua/studies/governance-genome-predicates-2026-04-05.md`
title: IRF-SYS-082: IMMUTABILITY predicate missing — governance-rules.json needs append-only mechanism

body:
## Seven Irreducible Governance Predicates

The minimal set of predicates such that any entity derived from base elements, at any scale, in any evolutionary configuration, remains logically bound to those base elements.

| # | Predicate | Prevents |
|---|-----------|----------|
| 1 | IDENTITY | Identity collision |
| 2 | TYPE | Semantic drift |
| 3 | EFFECT | Orphan existence |
| 4 | ACYCLICITY | Emergent cycles |
| 5 | COMPOSABILITY | Invalid composition at scale |
| 6 | FINITUDE | Lost derivation |
| 7 | IMMUTABILITY | Base mutation / path dependence |

Proven irreducible (removing any single predicate admits exactly one failure mode). Proven sufficient (all seven guarantee derivation exists, is valid, computable, verifiable, against stable base).

## Study

`praxis-perpetua/studies/governance-genome-predicates-2026-04-05.md`

## Implementation Audit

- IDENTITY: Partial (ontologia exists, not universal)
- TYPE: Partial (schemas validate format, not membership)
- EFFECT: Declared only (seed.yaml edges never verified)
- ACYCLICITY: Organ-level only
- COMPOSABILITY: Partial (state machine, not composition)
- FINITUDE: Hardcoded (max_transitive_depth=4)
- **IMMUTABILITY: MISSING ENTIRELY**

## Required Work

1. Assign SPEC number
2. Write formal definitions for each predicate
3. Define test criteria (what does enforcement look like?)
4. Plan scale propagation (predicates at every level, not just organ)
5. Implement IMMUTABILITY mechanism for governance-rules.json

## Related IRF Items

- IRF-SYS-082 (P1): IMMUTABILITY predicate missing
- IRF-SYS-083 (P2): Scale propagation gap
- IRF-SYS-084 (P2): EFFECT predicate unverified

## Session

S-genome (2026-04-05)
title: IRF-SYS-081: Formalize seven governance predicates as SPEC

## Summary

The **Triple Reference Principle** declares that every referenceable item must exist in exactly three locations simultaneously:

1. **IRF** — the universal work registry
2. **Repo** — as a tracked reference in the repo where the work lives
3. **GitHub Issue** — as an actionable ticket

Identity is composite, not assigned. The IRF ID is not a label stamped onto an item — it is the convergence artifact assembled from three locations coming together. Like a triangulation fix: no single station gives position. The position IS the intersection.

body:
## Nature demands documentation counterparts

`cultvra_logos.py` reports 16 mechanisms with no documentation counterpart. 72% coverage. Every mechanism that EXISTS must have a documentation counterpart that DESCRIBES it.

## Design

Each mechanism gets one `{mechanism}.md` file at root level. Two layers per document:

**Derived Layer** — Auto-extracted from gate contracts, cocoon-map.yaml, signal-graph.yaml. Regenerable.
- Functions (embodied + planned)
- Gate contracts (name, state, verb, signals)
- Signal flow (produces/consumes/defects)
- Absorption state (cocoon count, lines, source repos)

**Authored Layer** — Requires research per mechanism. Initially vacant.
- What this mechanism IS (ontological positioning)
- Critical lenses (Insider, Outsider, Adversary)
- Plotted work

**Spread** — The delta between derived and authored IS the documentation maturity metric.

Status progression: `DERIVED` → `TENDING` → `AUTHORED`

## Implementation

1. Write generation script to extract derived layer from existing data
2. Generate 16 `{mechanism}.md` files
3. Research + author ontological layer per mechanism (tracked by child issues)

## Plan

`.claude/plans/2026-04-03-mechanism-documentation-counterparts.md`

## Child Issues

16 mechanism-level issues track the authored layer research:

| Mechanism | Contracts | Cocoon Lines | Tier |

| skeletal | 2 | 5,626 | Rich (embodied) |

## Description

67 SOPs in praxis-perpetua govern operational procedures across the system, but there is no automated mechanism to detect when an SOP is outdated relative to the code it governs.

Additionally, some SOPs appear in the Active Directives table with both `system` and `unknown` scope (duplicates): pitch-deck-rollout, document-audit-feature-extraction, ira-grade-norming.

## Proposed

1. `organvm sop audit --stale` — compare SOP modification dates against governed code modification dates
2. Fix scope classification to prevent `unknown` duplicates in SOP discovery
3. Add SOP→code mapping in seed.yaml or a dedicated sop-governance.yaml

## Priority

P2 for staleness detection, P1 for the duplicate scope bug (it clutters every CLAUDE.md).

## Source

Documentation handoff architecture audit (2026-04-03).
title: feat: SOP staleness detection — cross-reference SOPs against governed code

body:
## Description

The exit interview protocol (`governance/exit_interview/testimony.py`) generates testimony via AST analysis, import graphs, and CLI entry point detection. This works well for code repos but produces thin testimony for documentation-only repos (praxis-perpetua, organvm-corpvs-testamentvm).

## Proposed

Extend testimony generation to support documentation artifacts:
- Word count, section structure, cross-reference density
- YAML/JSON schema coverage (for repos with governance data)
- Link graph analysis (internal and external references)
- SOP coverage mapping (which SOPs govern which code modules)

## Priority

P2 — the exit interview protocol is the most sophisticated handoff mechanism, but it has a blind spot for ~30% of the system.

## Source

Documentation handoff architecture audit (2026-04-03).
title: feat: exit interview testimony for documentation-only repos

body:
## Description

The Active Handoff Protocol (`.conductor/active-handoff.md`) has no expiration mechanism. A stale handoff file from weeks ago could mislead a new session. There is also no CLI to list all active handoffs across the workspace.

## Proposed

1. Add `created_at` and `expires_at` fields to handoff files
2. `organvm handoff list` — show all active handoff files across workspace with age
3. `organvm handoff clean [--older-than 7d]` — remove expired handoffs
4. Context sync should flag stale handoffs (>48h) in the generated context

## Priority

P2 — prevents phantom constraints from misleading agents.

## Source

Documentation handoff architecture audit (2026-04-03).
title: feat: handoff staleness detection and workspace-wide handoff listing

body:
## Description

`organvm context sync` currently broadcasts state into ~128 repos with no record of what changed between syncs. There is no diff, no changelog, and no rollback mechanism.

## Proposed

- On each sync, compute diff between old and new auto-generated sections
- Write a `context-sync-changelog.jsonl` (append-only) with per-file change records
- Add `organvm context diff [--last N]` to review recent changes
- Consider `organvm context rollback --to <timestamp>` for emergency recovery

## Priority

P2 — the system works without this, but debugging sync-related issues requires manual git archaeology across 128 repos.

## Source

Documentation handoff architecture audit (2026-04-03).
title: feat: context sync diff/changelog between runs
